data.narrative:
Isar Aerospace is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $400M+ against a current valuation of $1.0B+. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, with 4 same-stage private comparables directly applicable to the valuation.

Two quantitative methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implies a $1.7B valuation from the $1.0B last post-money round. Peer-median anchoring across 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies—Axiom Space ($2.5B), Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), and Skyryse ($1.1B)—yields a median of $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring was not applicable due to the absence of disclosed revenue figures. Both applicable methods converge tightly, supporting a triangulated valuation of $1.7B (median), bounded by $1.4B (low) and $2.0B (high). Key uncertainties include near-term revenue trajectory, competitive position within the sector, and capital efficiency relative to the peer set, particularly given the wide dispersion observed in comparable valuations.
